name: Neha Kapoor
tagline: Life is too short to overthink a ₹999 purchase

demographics.occupation: Brand Manager at a consumer goods MNC

aspiration_drivers: treating herself because she works hard
rejection_triggers: too many steps or effort required
products that feel boring or utilitarian

backstory: Neha works hard during the week — 10-hour days at a demanding MNC job — and shops hard on weekends. Her apartment has three unopened packages from last month and she does not feel guilty about it. Shopping is how she decompresses. She has a Nykaa wishlist with 47 items and her Myntra cart is never empty. She makes purchase decisions in under 60 seconds — if the packaging is pretty and the price is under ₹1500, it is in the cart. She has returned maybe two things in her life. Her bank statement is a diary of micro-indulgences.
current_life_context: She just got a performance bonus and is treating herself to a 'haul weekend.' She has already added six things across Myntra, Nykaa, and Amazon. Her best friend just sent her a Reel of a new lipstick shade that is 'literally perfect for you' and she is about to buy it without checking a single review.
inner_monologue_style: Rapid-fire, emotionally driven, zero friction. Thinks: 'Oh my god this is SO cute — add to cart. Wait how much? ₹799? Done. Ok what else? Ooh this has 4.5 stars, perfect. Also I need new earrings. And this face mask looks amazing — yolo.' Does not deliberate. Regret, if any, comes three days later and lasts about 10 minutes.
